source.description: Backdated launch lesson raster sketch created as the benchmark daily tutorial.
source.notes: The process frames use the same frog pose, umbrella arc, boots, and color placement as the finished image.

process_strategy: Build the frog from large guide shapes, then add the face, umbrella, arms, handle, boots, and final rainy-day color without changing the pose.

final_elements.name: wide umbrella arc
final_elements.description: The broad umbrella canopy above the frog.

final_elements.name: rounded frog head
final_elements.description: The wide head shape under the eyes.

final_elements.name: eye circles
final_elements.description: The two large eye circles on top of the head.

final_elements.name: oval frog body
final_elements.description: The centered body oval beneath the head.

final_elements.name: boot blocks
final_elements.description: The two blocky lower shapes for rain boots.

final_elements.name: smiling frog face
final_elements.description: The eye rings, smile, and cheek spots.

final_elements.name: umbrella ribs
final_elements.description: The curved rib lines dividing the umbrella canopy.

final_elements.name: arms and hands
final_elements.description: The small arms wrapping around the handle.

final_elements.name: tilted umbrella handle
final_elements.description: The handle running through the frog pose.

final_elements.name: finished rain boots
final_elements.description: The boot openings, rounded toes, and dark sole lines.

frames.visible_job: Places the frog head, eye circles, oval body, boot blocks, and umbrella arc before adding expression or detail.
Turns the head and eyes into a readable frog face while adding the umbrella ribs above the same body guides.
Adds the frog body contour, arms, hands, and tilted umbrella handle through the pose.
Converts the blocky feet into oversized rain boots with openings, toes, and sole lines.

final_step.visible_job: Adds the final green frog, yellow boots, blue umbrella, rain details, and keeper contours without changing the silhouette.

rejection_checks: The umbrella must stay wider than the frog in every frame.
The boot shapes must be introduced before the final color step.
The face, handle, and hands must line up with the finished pose.
The final color should remain loose colored pencil, not a polished fill.
